thought.text:
The `main` function is missing! The issue is likely with the `InitListExpr` hitting ABORT which causes the `main` function generation to crash mid-way. Let me fix InitListExpr and the `!` unary operator handling:

Actually, wait - looking at the score output, `arr_defn3` and `unary_op` are marked "运行输出结果时出错", not "output.ll missing". Let me check if the main function name is correct in the generated IR for these cases.

The "undefined reference to `main`" suggests the function name in our IR doesn't match. Let me check

action.command: cd /workspace/YatCC && grep "^define" build/test/task3/functional-3/049_unary_op.sysu.c/output.ll 2>/dev/null | head -5; echo "---"; grep "main" build/test/task3/functional-3/049_unary_op.sysu.c/output.ll 2>/dev/null | head -10

tool_call.name: terminal
tool_call.arguments: {"command": "cd /workspace/YatCC && grep \"^define\" build/test/task3/functional-3/049_unary_op.sysu.c/output.ll 2>/dev/null | head -5; echo \"---\"; grep \"main\" build/test/task3/functional-3/049_unary_op.sysu.c/output.ll 2>/dev/null | head -10", "summary": "Check function names in IR for failing cases"}

summary: Check function names in IR for failing cases
